channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Curry scores 34 points, Butler adds 25 in Golden State debut as Warriors rally to beat Bulls 132-111
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Stephen Curry scored 34 points and tied a season high with eight 3-pointers, Jimmy Butler added 25 points in his Golden State debut and the Warriors rallied to beat the Chicago Bulls 132-111 on Saturday night.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW SELIGMAN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Ben Simmons waived by the Nets after the team and former No. 1 pick agree to a buyout
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Ben Simmons was waived by the Brooklyn Nets on Saturday after the team agreed to a buyout with the last remaining link to a failed era of building around All-Stars. The No.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN MAHONEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Increased security in New Orleans ahead of Super Bowl
item.description: More than 125,000 visitors — including President Donald Trump — are expected to converge on the Big Easy this week.
item.dc:creator: Xavier Walton, NewsNation

item.title: Virginia Halas McCaskey, longtime Bears owner and matriarch, dies at 102, team announces
item.description: LAKE FOREST, Ill. — Virginia Halas McCaskey, the longtime owner and matriarch of the Chicago Bears, died Thursday at 102, the team announced in a news release. Virginia served as the owner of the Bears since her father and team founder George S. Halas died on Oct. 31, 1983.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: Penguins at the Birch Aquarium make their Super Bowl predictions
item.description: Some penguins at the Birch Aquarium at University of California, San Diego's Scripps Institution of Oceanography made their bets on which team will win the big game on Super Bowl Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Rhea Caoile, Fox 5

item.title: Three UPenn teammates of Lia Thomas sue university
item.description: Three former teammates of transgender swimmer Lia Thomas sued the University of Pennsylvania over rights violations
item.dc:creator: Rich McHugh, NewsNation

item.title: Plateau Valley’s Bevan Brothers Commit to Colorado Mesa
item.description: GRAND JUNCTION, Colo. (KREX) — Two brothers from Plateau Valley High School are taking the next step in their athletic careers, and they won’t have to go far to do it. Jackson and Heston Bevan, both standout multi-sport athletes, are staying close to home.
item.dc:creator: Titus Cleveland, KREX 5

item.title: Sick kids experience Super Bowl via innovative tech
item.description: Pediatric patients are able to control these robots, allowing them to participate in various Super Bowl events remotely.
item.dc:creator: Austin Kellerman, NewsNation

item.title: Artist explains how New Orleans childhood inspired Super Bowl logo
item.description: Months before the Chiefs and Eagles earned a ticket to Super Bowl LIX, the NFL design team asked Tajh to create both theme art and the logo for Super Bowl 59.
item.dc:creator: Amy Russo, NewsNation

item.title: Boys Basketball: Tri City Christian 49, Army Navy 37
item.description: SAN DIEGO (KUSI)- Great game on Monday night in Vista with Tri City Christian hosting Army Navy. All tied up at 21 at halftime, the Eagles pulled off and won 49-37. Lead by Caleb Williams who hit for 14. Army Navy was lead by Yanler Ramos, he also poured in 14.
item.dc:creator: Nick James, Fox 5

item.title: 70 Illinois residents can win Las Vegas vacation with Rosati's Pizza
item.description: Illinois residents can enter the Rosati's Pizza sweepstakes and win a three-day, two-night vacation at the Circa Resort and Casino in Las Vegas, with the contest closing on the day of the Big Game.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Influencers, retired Bay Area sports stars headline NBA All-Star Celebrity Game in Oakland
item.description: (KRON) -- The NBA released the list of celebrities that will take the floor in Oakland for the NBA All-Star Celebrity Game on Feb. 14. A combination of influencers, retired athletes, film stars and more will put their basketball skills to test.
item.dc:creator: Phil Mayer, KRON4

item.title: Former NFL player Rashard Cook looks to shape next generation at Mater Dei
item.description: "We had great experiences past high school, and I want to recreate that for the guys that want that experience.
item.dc:creator: Brandon Stone, Fox 5

item.title: Report: Bears GM Ryan Poles expected to receive 3-year contract extension, according to team source
item.description: The Bears are expected to extend general manager Ryan Poles' contract for three more years, in order to align it with the contract signed by new head coach Ben Johnson, according to a feature story this week by ESPN Bears reporter Courtney Cronin. Cronin published a feature story Monday at ESPN.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: Major League Baseball fires ump amid gambling probe
item.description: Umpire Pat Hoberg was fired after it was revealed he shared betting accounts with a friend who bet on baseball.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, WGN 9

item.title: Luka Doncic heads to the Lakers and Anthony Davis goes to the Mavs in blockbuster trade
item.description: The trade was put together in secret, with only a precious few people knowing what was happening. Luka Doncic didn't know. Anthony Davis didn't know. Their coaches didn't know. Their teammates didn't know. And then it happened — an absolute blockbuster.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Canadian fans boo U.S. National Anthem at hockey game amid trade war
item.description: Canadians aren’t too happy with the tariffs being imposed on their country by U.S. President Donald Trump. Trump signed an order Saturday to impose stiff tariffs on imports from Canada as well as Mexico and China, which drew swift retaliation and sparked a trade war.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, KTLA 5

item.title: Ohtani's return to pitching could come in May for World Series champion Dodgers
item.description:
Shohei Ohtani pitching for the Los Angeles Dodgers in May is "about right,” manager Dave Roberts said Saturday at the team's annual fan fest.
He reiterated that Ohtani won't pitch in the season-opening Japan Series against the Chicago Cubs on March 18-19 in Tokyo.
item.dc:creator: BETH HARRIS, Associated Press, Fox 5
